titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Geoffrey O'Hara cylinder recordings and lantern slides of Navajo Indians
O'Hara (Geoffrey) cylinder recordings and lantern slides of Navajo Indians
titlestmt.author: Beinecke Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday August 19, 2020 at 05:30

did.unittitle: Geoffrey O'Hara cylinder recordings and lantern slides of Navajo Indians

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

physdescstructured.physfacet: circa 135 rpm, mono ; 6 cm x 11 cm
Lantern slides : glass, b & w and color ; 8.3 x 11 cm.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Sound recordings are restricted fragile material. Reference copies are available. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from William Reese Co. on the Walter McClintock Memorial Fund, 2009.

bioghist.head: Geoffrey O'Hara
bioghist.p: Geoffrey O'Hara (1882-1967) was a Canadian American composer, singer and music teacher.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Cylinder recordings of Navajo Indian songs, probably complied by Geoffrey O'Hara, and lantern slides of Navajo Indians in Arizona, circa 1913-1914. The recordings consist chiefly of songs related to the Navajo Yeibichai ceremony. Singers include Robert Dayea, Shay Milford, and Miguelito (also known as Red Point). Lantern slides include informal portraits of Navajo men, women, and children in Fort Defiance and at the Hubbell Trading Post in Ganado. The collection also includes a portrait of O'Hara with Denver, a Shoshoni Indian man. Images include Navajo Indian ceremonies and dances, as well as foot and horse races. Images of Arizona include views of desert landscapes and arroyos with sheep and goats, as well as a view of the White House Ruins at Canyon de Chelly. Copy photographs in the collection include a portrait of Navajo Chief Manuelito, circa 1874, and illustrations of Navajo Yeibichai masks from Washington Matthews's anthopological study, Night Chant: A Navaho Ceremony (1902).

did.unittitle: Lantern slides

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lantern slides include informal portraits of Navajo men, women, and children in Fort Defiance and at the Hubbell Trading Post in Ganado. The collection also includes a portrait of O'Hara with Denver, a Shoshoni Indian man. Images include Navajo Indian ceremonies and dances, as well as foot and horse races. Images of Arizona include views of desert landscapes and arroyos with sheep and goats, as well as a view of the White House Ruins at Canyon de Chelly.

did.unittitle: Navajo children in Western dress, seated in a line, possibly outside their school house in Fort Defiance
Group of Navajo boys at a distance, possibly on the town green area in Fort Defiance
Unidentified desert scene with tipi made of sticks
Image of music for two tunes, both titled Navajo Dance Song
Image of music for two tunes titled Navajo War Song and Navajo Medicine Song
Two very early cars in a desert landscape, one broken down
Several Indian boys racing on horseback, watched by a crowd of people both on foot and horseback, in a desert landscape
Group of people watching an Indian dance, possibly a Hopi Snake Dance
Clothed skeleton in an opened coffin
Group of eight Indians, possibly preparing for a ceremony of some kind, in a desert landscape
Same group of eight Indians, possibly preparing for a ceremony of some kind, in a desert landscape from a different angle
Four Indian boys prepared to race on foot, watched by a large group of men on horseback
Another group of six Indian boys preparing for a footrace, watched by a large group of men on horseback
Brick and log hogan-esque structure in a desert landscape
Two white men standing outside the door to the Hubbell Trading Post at Ganado, Az
View of a group of pueblos on a ridge, taken from a fair distance. People and horses at the foot of the ridge
Navajo man on horseback
A man and two donkeys outside Hubbell Trading Post
Group of mounted men, milling about
View of a desert rock formation
A woman and several horses in a desert landscape possibly near Hubbell Trading Post
The same two men as featured in photo (16), still standing in front of Hubbell, but this image taken from further away
View of the White House Ruins at Canyon de Chelly
Indian man in a uniform doffing his cap at an Indian couple, similarly attired likely in the town of Ft Defiance
Group of turkeys in some grass, possibly on a riverbank; desert landscape in the background
Two Model T Fords, circa 1915, with white passengers; five in one car and six in the other (three passengers are children)
View of an unidentified canyon wall
View of an unidentified desert landscape
A man mounted on a donkey, with another donkey in the foreground, outside of Hubbell Trading Post Two wagons heaped high with goods are parked in front of the building
Another group of six Indian boys preparing for a footrace, watched by a large group of men on horseback
Landscape
Sheep and goats in an arroyo
Two women and a dog standing in front of a flock of goats and sheep
Group of eight Navajo school girls in Western dress and smiling, possibly on the town green area in Ft. Defiance
Copy photograph of a Navajo man
Copy photograph of Navajo Chief Manuelito, circa 1874

Copy photograph of a drawing of a Navajo Yeibichai mask from Washington Matthews (1983-1905), Night Chant, a Navaho ceremony in Memoirs of the American Museum of Natural History, Volume 6 New York: The Knickerbocker Press, 1902

Copy photograph of a drawing of a Navajo Yeibichai mask from Washington Matthews (1983-1905), Night Chant, a Navaho ceremony in Memoirs of the American Museum of Natural History, Volume 6 New York: The Knickerbocker Press, 1902

Copy photograph of a drawing of a Navajo Yeibichai mask from Washington Matthews (1983-1905), Night Chant, a Navaho ceremony in Memoirs of the American Museum of Natural History, Volume 6 New York: The Knickerbocker Press, 1902
Sheep and goats near an arroyo
Two men in front of a large group of mounted men
A Navajo man, two women, two boys, and a girl with machine made blankets
A canyon with an arroyo
Exterior portrait of Geoffrey O'Hara and Denver, a Shoshone man
Group of Navajo boys, probably on the town green in Fort Defiance
Cylinder recordings

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Sound recordings are restricted fragile material. Reference copies are available. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The recordings consist chiefly of songs related to the Navajo Yeibichai ceremony. Singers include Robert Dayea, Shay Milford, and Miguelito (also known as Red Point).

accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains three songs, "Song of the Coyote", "Bird Song", and an alternate version of "Bird Song".
